contract_types.name: NDA
contract_types.description: Non-disclosure agreement governing the exchange of confidential information between two or more parties.

clauses.name: Definition of Confidential Information
clauses.description: Defines what information is treated as confidential and what is expressly excluded (e.g. already public, independently developed).
negotiation_points: scope of what counts as confidential

clauses.name: Term and Duration
clauses.description: States how long the agreement, and the confidentiality obligation itself, remains in force.
negotiation_points: length of the confidentiality survival period after termination

clauses.name: Permitted Use
clauses.description: Limits use of the disclosed information to an agreed purpose only.

clauses.name: Return or Destruction of Information
clauses.description: Requires the receiving party to return or destroy confidential information at the end of the relationship.

clauses.name: Non-Solicitation
clauses.description: Restricts one party from soliciting the other's employees or customers — a common source of unbalanced risk if scoped too broadly.
negotiation_points: duration and scope of the restriction

clauses.name: Remedies and Injunctive Relief
clauses.description: Confirms that a breach may cause irreparable harm and entitles the disclosing party to seek injunctive relief in addition to damages.

contract_types.name: vendor agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement governing the supply of goods or services by a vendor to the organization.

clauses.name: Scope of Services
clauses.description: Defines precisely what the vendor is obligated to deliver.

clauses.name: Service Levels
clauses.description: Sets measurable performance standards (e.g. uptime, response times) and remedies for missing them.

clauses.name: Data Protection and Confidentiality
clauses.description: Obligates the vendor to protect the organization's data and confidential information to a defined standard.

clauses.name: Audit Rights
clauses.description: Gives the organization the right to audit the vendor's compliance with the agreement, including security controls.

clauses.name: Indemnification
clauses.description: Allocates responsibility for third-party claims arising from the vendor's performance — an unbalanced indemnity can expose the organization to open-ended liability.

clauses.name: Limitation of Liability
clauses.description: Caps each party's financial exposure under the agreement — a low cap can leave the organization under-protected relative to its actual exposure.
negotiation_points: cap amount relative to contract value
exclusions from the cap (e.g. confidentiality breach, gross negligence)

clauses.name: Termination for Convenience
clauses.description: Allows either party to end the agreement without cause on notice.

contract_types.name: SaaS agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement governing subscription access to a vendor's cloud-hosted software.

clauses.name: Service Availability and Uptime
clauses.description: Commits the vendor to a minimum uptime percentage, typically backed by service credits.

clauses.name: Data Ownership
clauses.description: Confirms the organization retains ownership of its own data hosted in the service.

clauses.name: Data Security and Breach Notification
clauses.description: Requires defined security controls and a specific timeframe for notifying the organization of a data breach.

clauses.name: Subprocessors
clauses.description: Governs the vendor's use of its own subcontractors to process the organization's data — a source of hidden third-party risk if not disclosed and controlled.
negotiation_points: right to object to a new subprocessor

clauses.name: Limitation of Liability
clauses.description: Caps the vendor's liability for service failures or data incidents.

clauses.name: Termination and Data Return
clauses.description: Sets out how the organization's data is returned or deleted, and in what format, once the subscription ends.

clauses.name: Intellectual Property License
clauses.description: Clarifies that the organization receives only a license to use the software, not ownership of the underlying IP.

contract_types.name: employment contract
contract_types.description: Agreement governing the terms of an individual's employment with the organization.

clauses.name: Job Duties and Reporting Line
clauses.description: Describes the employee's role, responsibilities, and who they report to.

clauses.name: Compensation and Benefits
clauses.description: States salary, bonus structure, and benefits entitlements.

clauses.name: Confidentiality
clauses.description: Protects the organization's confidential information and trade secrets during and after employment.

clauses.name: Intellectual Property Assignment
clauses.description: Assigns to the organization any IP the employee creates within the scope of their employment.

clauses.name: Non-Compete
clauses.description: Restricts the employee from working for a competitor for a period after leaving — enforceability and scope vary significantly by jurisdiction.

clauses.name: Termination and Notice Period
clauses.description: Sets out how the employment relationship may be ended and the required notice.

contract_types.name: service agreement
contract_types.description: General agreement for the provision of professional or operational services.

clauses.name: Scope of Work
clauses.description: Defines the specific services to be performed, often via a statement of work.

clauses.name: Acceptance Criteria
clauses.description: Defines the standard the deliverables must meet before they are accepted and paid for.

clauses.name: Payment Terms
clauses.description: States pricing, invoicing schedule, and payment due dates.

clauses.name: Warranty
clauses.description: Warrants that the services will be performed to a professional standard and, if defective, will be corrected.

clauses.name: Limitation of Liability
clauses.description: Caps the provider's liability for losses arising from the services.
negotiation_points: exclusions for gross negligence or willful misconduct

clauses.name: Termination
clauses.description: Sets out the grounds and process for ending the agreement, including for breach.
negotiation_points: cure period before termination for breach

contract_types.name: procurement contract
contract_types.description: Agreement for the purchase of goods, equipment, or supplies.

clauses.name: Specifications and Deliverables
clauses.description: Defines the exact goods to be delivered, including quantity, quality, and technical specifications.

clauses.name: Pricing and Payment Terms
clauses.description: States unit pricing, total contract value, and payment schedule.
negotiation_points: price adjustment mechanism for long-term contracts

clauses.name: Delivery and Inspection
clauses.description: Sets delivery timelines, location, and the organization's right to inspect goods before acceptance.

clauses.name: Warranty and Quality
clauses.description: Warrants that delivered goods conform to specification and are free from defects for a defined period.

clauses.name: Indemnification
clauses.description: Allocates responsibility for third-party claims arising from defective or non-conforming goods.

clauses.name: Termination for Default
clauses.description: Allows the organization to terminate if the supplier fails to deliver conforming goods on time.
